cls:oxide.name: Oxides
cls:oxide.description: Binary compounds of an element with oxygen

cls:oxide_basic.name: Basic oxides
cls:oxide_basic.description: Metal oxides that react with acids to form a salt and water

cls:oxide_acidic.name: Acidic oxides
cls:oxide_acidic.description: Nonmetal oxides that react with bases to form a salt and water

cls:oxide_amphoteric.name: Amphoteric oxides
cls:oxide_amphoteric.description: Oxides that exhibit both basic and acidic properties

cls:oxide_indifferent.name: Indifferent oxides
cls:oxide_indifferent.description: Oxides that do not form salts with acids or bases

cls:acid.name: Acids
cls:acid.description: Compounds composed of hydrogen atoms and an acid residue

cls:acid_oxygen.name: Oxyacids
cls:acid_oxygen.description: Acids whose acid residue contains oxygen

cls:acid_oxygenfree.name: Binary acids
cls:acid_oxygenfree.description: Acids whose acid residue does not contain oxygen

cls:base.name: Bases
cls:base.description: Compounds composed of a metal cation and hydroxide anion OH⁻

cls:base_alkali.name: Alkalis
cls:base_alkali.description: Water-soluble bases formed by alkali and alkaline earth metals

cls:base_insoluble.name: Insoluble bases
cls:base_insoluble.description: Bases insoluble in water, decompose on heating

cls:base_amphoteric.name: Amphoteric bases
cls:base_amphoteric.description: Bases that exhibit both basic and acidic properties

cls:salt.name: Salts
cls:salt.description: Compounds composed of a metal cation and an acid residue anion

cls:salt_normal.name: Normal salts
cls:salt_normal.description: Salts in which all hydrogen atoms of the acid are replaced by a metal

cls:salt_acidic.name: Acidic salts
cls:salt_acidic.description: Salts in which not all hydrogen atoms of the acid are replaced by a metal

cls:salt_basic.name: Basic salts
cls:salt_basic.description: Salts in which not all hydroxyl groups of the base are replaced by an acid residue

prop:state_of_matter.name: State of matter
prop:state_of_matter.description: The form in which a substance exists, determined by the motion and interaction of its particles: solid, liquid, gas, plasma. Water is a classic example: ice (solid), liquid water, steam (gas).

prop:color.name: Color
prop:color.description: A property determined by the wavelengths of photons a substance reflects or emits. Depends on electronic structure: Cu(OH)₂ is blue, Fe(OH)₃ is brown, KMnO₄ is purple.

prop:odor.name: Odor
prop:odor.description: A property perceived by the sense of smell, determined by volatile molecules interacting with olfactory receptors. H₂S smells of rotten eggs; NH₃ has a sharp, pungent smell.

prop:density.name: Density
prop:density.description: Mass per unit volume of a substance (g/cm³ or kg/m³). Determines whether a substance sinks or floats, and depends on particle mass and packing efficiency.

prop:melting_point.name: Melting point
prop:melting_point.description: The temperature at which a substance transitions from solid to liquid at a given pressure. Depends on crystal lattice type and strength of interparticle bonds.

prop:boiling_point.name: Boiling point
prop:boiling_point.description: The temperature at which a substance's vapor pressure equals the external pressure and it transitions to a gaseous state. Water boils at 100 °C (at 1 atm).

prop:thermal_stability.name: Thermal stability
prop:thermal_stability.description: The ability of a substance to maintain its chemical composition and structure when heated. Insoluble bases and some salts (CaCO₃) decompose on heating; alkali hydroxides are thermally stable.

prop:flammability.name: Flammability
prop:flammability.description: The ability of a substance to undergo exothermic oxidation releasing heat and light (combustion). Depends on substance nature, ignition temperature, and oxidizer availability.

prop:atom.name: Atom
prop:atom.description: The smallest particle of a chemical element that retains its chemical properties. Consists of a positively charged nucleus (protons + neutrons) and an electron shell. Size is on the order of 10⁻¹⁰ m.

prop:molecule.name: Molecule
prop:molecule.description: An electrically neutral particle formed by two or more atoms joined by covalent bonds. The smallest particle of a substance that retains its chemical properties. H₂O, CO₂, O₂ are molecules.

prop:ion.name: Ion
prop:ion.description: An atom or group of atoms carrying an electric charge as a result of losing or gaining electrons. Cation (+) — loss of electrons; anion (−) — gain of electrons.

prop:crystal_lattice.name: Crystal lattice
prop:crystal_lattice.description: An ordered three-dimensional arrangement of particles (atoms, molecules, or ions) in a solid. The lattice type (ionic, atomic, molecular, metallic) determines physical properties: melting point, hardness, brittleness.

prop:oxidizer.name: Oxidizing agent
prop:oxidizer.description: An atom, molecule, or ion that accepts electrons in a redox reaction. The oxidation state of the oxidizing agent decreases (it is reduced).

prop:reducer.name: Reducing agent
prop:reducer.description: An atom, molecule, or ion that donates electrons in a redox reaction. The oxidation state of the reducing agent increases (it is oxidized).

concept:atomic_mass.name: Atomic mass
concept:atomic_mass.description: Mass of an atom expressed in atomic mass units

concept:electronegativity.name: Electronegativity
concept:electronegativity.description: Ability of an atom to attract electron density in a chemical bond

concept:melting_point.name: Melting point
concept:melting_point.description: Temperature at which a substance changes from solid to liquid

concept:boiling_point.name: Boiling point
concept:boiling_point.description: Temperature at which a substance's vapour pressure equals atmospheric pressure

concept:density.name: Density
concept:density.description: Ratio of a substance's mass to its volume

concept:ion_charge.name: Ion charge
concept:ion_charge.description: Electric charge of an ion in elementary charge units

concept:molar_mass.name: Molar mass
concept:molar_mass.description: Mass of one mole of a substance

concept:enthalpy_of_formation.name: Enthalpy of formation
concept:enthalpy_of_formation.description: Standard enthalpy of forming a substance from its elements

concept:standard_entropy.name: Standard entropy
concept:standard_entropy.description: Measure of a system's disorder under standard conditions

concept:pKa.name: pKa
concept:pKa.description: Negative base-10 logarithm of the acid dissociation constant

concept:acid_dissociation_constant.name: Acid dissociation constant
concept:acid_dissociation_constant.description: Equilibrium constant for acid dissociation in aqueous solution. The larger Ka, the stronger the acid

concept:acid_strength.name: Acid strength
concept:acid_strength.description: Ability of an acid to donate a proton, determined by its dissociation constant

concept:acid_basicity.name: Acid basicity
concept:acid_basicity.description: Number of protons an acid can donate upon full dissociation

concept:acid_dissociation.name: Acid dissociation
concept:acid_dissociation.description: Process of acid breaking into ions in aqueous solution

concept:conjugate_base.name: Conjugate base
concept:conjugate_base.description: Particle formed when an acid donates a proton

concept:acid_residue.name: Acid residue
concept:acid_residue.description: Anion formed upon full dissociation of an acid

concept:indicator.name: Indicator
concept:indicator.description: Substance that changes colour depending on the solution's environment

concept:bond_strength.name: Bond strength
concept:bond_strength.description: Energy required to break a chemical bond

cls:acid_strong.name: Strong acids
cls:acid_strong.description: Acids that dissociate completely in aqueous solution (pKa < 0)

cls:acid_weak.name: Weak acids
cls:acid_weak.description: Acids that dissociate partially in aqueous solution (pKa > 0)

rxtype:acid_metal.name: Acid + metal
rxtype:acid_metal.description: Reaction of acid with a metal above hydrogen in the activity series

rxtype:acid_base_oxide.name: Acid + basic oxide
rxtype:acid_base_oxide.description: Reaction of acid with a basic oxide

rxtype:acid_carbonate.name: Acid + carbonate
rxtype:acid_carbonate.description: Reaction of acid with a carbonate, releasing CO₂

concept:acid_composition.name: By composition
concept:acid_composition.description: Classification of acids by the presence of oxygen

concept:oxide_character.name: By character
concept:oxide_character.description: Classification of oxides by acid-base properties

concept:base_solubility.name: By solubility
concept:base_solubility.description: Classification of bases by water solubility

concept:salt_composition.name: By composition
concept:salt_composition.description: Classification of salts by degree of hydrogen/hydroxide substitution

concept:chemical_bond.name: Chemical bond
concept:chemical_bond.description: An interaction between atoms that holds them together in molecules, crystals, and other structures. Types include ionic, covalent, and metallic bonds.

concept:ionic_bond.name: Ionic bond
concept:ionic_bond.description: A bond formed by the electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions, created when electrons are transferred from a metal atom to a nonmetal atom.

concept:covalent_bond.name: Covalent bond
concept:covalent_bond.description: A bond formed by the sharing of electron pairs between atoms. Can be polar or nonpolar depending on the electronegativity difference.

concept:covalent_polar_bond.name: Polar covalent bond
concept:covalent_polar_bond.description: A covalent bond between atoms of different electronegativities, where the shared electron pair is shifted toward the more electronegative atom.

concept:covalent_nonpolar_bond.name: Nonpolar covalent bond
concept:covalent_nonpolar_bond.description: A covalent bond between atoms of the same element, where the shared electron pair is distributed symmetrically.

concept:metallic_bond.name: Metallic bond
concept:metallic_bond.description: A bond in metals and alloys formed by the sharing of valence electrons that move freely among metal cations in a crystal lattice.

concept:ionic_crystal_lattice.name: Ionic crystal lattice
concept:ionic_crystal_lattice.description: A crystal lattice with alternating cations and anions at lattice points. Typical for salts, bases, and metal oxides. Substances have high melting and boiling points.

concept:molecular_crystal_lattice.name: Molecular crystal lattice
concept:molecular_crystal_lattice.description: A crystal lattice with molecules at lattice points, held together by weak intermolecular forces. Substances have low melting points and are volatile.

concept:atomic_crystal_lattice.name: Atomic crystal lattice
concept:atomic_crystal_lattice.description: A crystal lattice with atoms at lattice points, connected by strong covalent bonds. Substances (diamond, silicon, SiO2) are very hard, refractory, and insoluble.

concept:metallic_crystal_lattice.name: Metallic crystal lattice
concept:metallic_crystal_lattice.description: A crystal lattice with metal cations at lattice points surrounded by delocalized electrons. Provides electrical and thermal conductivity, malleability, and metallic luster.

concept:shared_electron_pair.name: Shared electron pair
concept:shared_electron_pair.description: A pair of electrons shared between two atoms, forming a covalent bond.

concept:partial_charge.name: Partial charge
concept:partial_charge.description: A charge (delta+, delta-) that appears on atoms in a polar covalent bond due to uneven distribution of electron density.

concept:delocalized_electron.name: Delocalized electron
concept:delocalized_electron.description: An electron not bound to a specific atom, moving freely throughout the metal crystal lattice. Responsible for the electrical and thermal conductivity of metals.

concept:electron_transfer.name: Electron transfer
concept:electron_transfer.description: The process of one or more electrons moving from one atom to another, resulting in ion formation and ionic bonding.

concept:electron_density.name: Electron density
concept:electron_density.description: The probability distribution of electrons in a molecule. In a polar bond, electron density is shifted toward the more electronegative atom.

concept:bond_polarity.name: Bond polarity
concept:bond_polarity.description: A property of a covalent bond determined by the electronegativity difference between the bonded atoms. The greater the difference, the more polar the bond.

concept:solid_phase.name: Solid phase
concept:solid_phase.description: A state of matter characterized by an ordered arrangement of particles and the retention of both shape and volume.

concept:liquid_phase.name: Liquid phase
concept:liquid_phase.description: A state of matter that retains volume but lacks a fixed shape. Particles are mobile yet held together by intermolecular forces.

concept:gaseous_phase.name: Gaseous phase
concept:gaseous_phase.description: A state of matter with neither fixed volume nor fixed shape. Particles move randomly and interact very weakly.

concept:metals.name: Metals
concept:metals.description: Class of simple substances characterized by electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity, malleability/ductility, and metallic luster.

concept:nonmetals.name: Nonmetals
concept:nonmetals.description: Class of simple substances that are typically poor conductors, brittle in solid form.

concept:metalloids.name: Metalloids
concept:metalloids.description: Class of simple substances with intermediate properties between metals and nonmetals; typical semiconductors.

grp:transition_metal.name: Transition metals
grp:transition_metal.description: d-block elements with partially filled d-subshells.

grp:post_transition_metal.name: Post-transition metals
grp:post_transition_metal.description: p-block metals located after the transition metals in the periodic table.

grp:metalloid.name: Metalloids (group)
grp:metalloid.description: Group of elements with intermediate properties between metals and nonmetals.

concept:electrical_conductivity.name: Electrical conductivity
concept:electrical_conductivity.description: The ability of a substance to conduct electric current. Determined by the presence of free charged particles — electrons or ions. Metals exhibit high electrical conductivity due to delocalized electrons.

concept:thermal_conductivity.name: Thermal conductivity
concept:thermal_conductivity.description: The ability of a substance to transfer thermal energy from hotter regions to cooler ones. In metals, the main mechanism is energy transfer by free electrons.

concept:hardness.name: Hardness
concept:hardness.description: The ability of a material to resist local plastic deformation under indentation. Hardness depends on the type of chemical bond and crystal lattice: substances with atomic crystal lattices are usually very hard.

concept:brittleness.name: Brittleness
concept:brittleness.description: The tendency of a material to fracture without significant plastic deformation. Substances with ionic bonds are often brittle because displacement of ion layers leads to electrostatic repulsion and fracture.

concept:plasticity.name: Plasticity
concept:plasticity.description: The ability of a material to undergo irreversible deformation without fracture. Metals are ductile due to metallic bonding: delocalized electrons allow atomic layers to slide without losing cohesion.

concept:metallic_luster.name: Metallic luster
concept:metallic_luster.description: The characteristic shine of metal surfaces caused by the reflection of light by delocalized electrons in the metallic crystal lattice.

concept:force.name: Force
concept:force.description: A measure of interaction between bodies; a physical quantity characterizing the action of one body on another, causing a change in velocity or deformation.

concept:interaction.name: Interaction
concept:interaction.description: The mutual action of objects upon each other, leading to changes in their state. In chemistry, electrostatic interaction of charged particles plays a key role.

concept:particle.name: Particle
concept:particle.description: A small-scale object — atom, molecule, ion, or electron — whose behavior determines the properties of matter at the microscopic level.

concept:charge.name: Electric charge
concept:charge.description: A fundamental physical quantity that determines electromagnetic interaction. Charge carriers — electrons and ions — enable electrical conductivity and chemical bond formation.

concept:field.name: Field
concept:field.description: A physical entity existing at all points in space, characterized by a specific physical quantity at each point. An electric field is created by electric charges.

concept:electric_field.name: Electric field
concept:electric_field.description: A force field created by electric charges. It acts on charged particles with a force proportional to their charge. It determines electrostatic interaction in ionic bonds.

concept:electrostatic_interaction.name: Electrostatic interaction
concept:electrostatic_interaction.description: Interaction between electric charges. In ionic bonds — attraction between cations and anions. The strength of the interaction is determined by the magnitude of charges and the distance between them.

concept:deformation.name: Deformation
concept:deformation.description: A change in the shape or size of a body under the action of external forces. Elastic (reversible) and plastic (irreversible) deformation are distinguished.

concept:plastic_deformation.name: Plastic deformation
concept:plastic_deformation.description: Irreversible change in body shape that persists after load removal. Metals readily undergo plastic deformation due to metallic bonding.

concept:fracture.name: Fracture
concept:fracture.description: The process of a body separating into parts under external forces. Brittle fracture occurs without significant plastic deformation.

concept:cation.name: Cation
concept:cation.description: A positively charged ion formed when an atom loses one or more electrons.

concept:anion.name: Anion
concept:anion.description: A negatively charged ion formed when an atom gains one or more electrons.

concept:chemical_reaction.name: Chemical reaction
concept:chemical_reaction.description: A process of transformation of substances into others with changes in their composition and structure.

concept:dissolution.name: Dissolution
concept:dissolution.description: A physical-chemical process of a substance transitioning into solution.

concept:crystallization.name: Crystallization
concept:crystallization.description: The process of crystal formation from a solution, melt, or gas phase. The reverse of dissolution.

concept:electrolysis.name: Electrolysis
concept:electrolysis.description: An oxidation-reduction process at electrodes driven by direct electric current through an electrolyte solution or melt.

concept:complexation.name: Complexation
concept:complexation.description: Formation of a complex ion from a central atom (metal ion) and ligands.

concept:adsorption.name: Adsorption
concept:adsorption.description: Uptake of a substance from solution or gas phase by the surface layer of a solid (adsorbent).

concept:sublimation.name: Sublimation
concept:sublimation.description: Phase transition from solid directly to gas, bypassing the liquid phase.

concept:condensation.name: Condensation
concept:condensation.description: Phase transition from gas to liquid. The reverse of evaporation.

concept:photodecomposition.name: Photodecomposition
concept:photodecomposition.description: Decomposition of a substance under the action of light.

concept:gas_absorption.name: Gas absorption
concept:gas_absorption.description: Physical-chemical process of gas transitioning into a liquid phase (solution).

concept:oxidation.name: Oxidation
concept:oxidation.description: Process of electron loss by an atom, ion, or molecule. Oxidation state increases.

concept:reduction.name: Reduction
concept:reduction.description: Process of electron gain by an atom, ion, or molecule. Oxidation state decreases.

concept:electrode.name: Electrode
concept:electrode.description: A conductor through which electric current enters or leaves an electrolyte.

concept:cathode.name: Cathode
concept:cathode.description: Electrode at which reduction occurs. Negative electrode during electrolysis.

concept:anode.name: Anode
concept:anode.description: Electrode at which oxidation occurs. Positive electrode during electrolysis.

concept:electrolyte.name: Electrolyte
concept:electrolyte.description: Substance that dissociates into ions in solution or melt, conducting electric current.

concept:electric_current.name: Electric current
concept:electric_current.description: Directed flow of charged particles through a conductor.
